phase3_session_1_fingerprint_note: Verified clean: fingerprint --verify --check-broker passed before session, frozen hashes matched, working tree clean, 0 open orders, 0 open positions at start and shutdown.

phase3_fingerprint._note: Frozen at Phase 3 readiness commit. SHA check uses CAPTURE_AFTER_PUSH sentinel: a committed file cannot contain the SHA of the commit that holds it (self-referential hash). Integrity is enforced by the four file hashes below plus clean working tree. Record the frozen commit SHA via: git tag phase3-baseline && git push origin phase3-baseline. Regenerate hashes with: python scripts/capture_session_fingerprint.py. A session whose runtime fingerprint diverges from this baseline is NOT eligible for the Phase 3 clean cohort without a declared protocol amendment.

_broker_hash_amendment.reason: 11 defect fixes applied between Session 1 and Session 2 (commit 37f8f1a). alpaca_broker.py changed for Fix 10: option quote timestamp now parsed from exchange latestQuote.t instead of datetime.utcnow(). No threshold, parameter, or strategy logic changes. Other behavioral fixes (RVOL pace projection, EOD entry cutoff, signal age gate, scanner direction match, exit bid pricing, ORB unified definition, delta hard rejection, IV filter dead code, ledger breakeven/profit-factor/delta-bucket, replay fixed strike, VWAP lookahead, backtest EOD exit) are all defect corrections. Session 1 was valid under the then-current baseline. Sessions 2+ run under this updated baseline. Cross-session comparison should note the code boundary at S1/S2.

phase3_fingerprint.db_schema_note: DBTradeJournal schema v2 (phase/contamination_flags columns added); see app/trading/db_journal.py

note: Phase 3 cohort. All sessions on or after 2026-07-12 with all P1-P7 fixes applied. Pre-phase-3 sessions (P1/P2 phases) are engineering evidence only — see evaluation/ledger.json contamination_flags.

fill_detail.exit_fill_note: $0.47 fill against $0.45 sell limit is valid price improvement

phase3_sessions.contamination_note: Both fills recovered via reconciler (stale-cancel pattern): PLTR limit order filled just before 5-min cancel window; XLE limit order filled just before cancel (422 response). Broker-reported fill prices are authoritative. Session ended clean: 0 positions, 0 orders, api_errors=0. Reconciler warning 'PM has PLTR but broker does not' is a timing artifact — PLTR had been sold at broker before PM processed closure. Not a data integrity issue.
phase3_sessions.reconciliation_warning_detail: Timing artifact only: PM held stale PLTR reference after broker-confirmed close. Resolved by EOD.

cumulative.note: Phase 3 clean cohort only. Sessions 1-2. Code boundary at S1/S2: 11 defect fixes applied.

Q1_orb_vs_vwap.note: 1 ORB trade (+$83), 2 VWAP trades (-$1, -$11). Preliminary: ORB outperforming but N=1 for ORB.

Q3_dte.note: DTE-4: 1 trade (-$1). DTE-3: 2 trades (+$72 net). Preliminary only.

Q7_fill_path.note: Both S2 fills via reconciler_recovery. Stale-cancel window (5 min) too tight — systematic pattern requiring fix.

open_issues.description: Both S2 fills occurred just before the 5-minute stale-cancel timer fired. The reconciler recovers them correctly but introduces a 25-30 minute RECON_BLOCKED period and adds timing noise to entry price vs intended limit. The 5-minute window should be extended (suggest 10-15 min) or the 422-cancel response should immediately trigger a reconciler check rather than waiting for the next poll cycle.
